updates.title: Dense graph cells no longer skip collision pairs
updates.summary: A real CodeGraphy-monorepo screenshot exposed the remaining collision defect: the spatial-grid pass stopped after 128 nearby entries, so dense cells could skip a large/small pair and still report settlement. The arbitrary cap is removed and a >128-candidate zoom-scaled regression protects the complete neighborhood scan. Clean 500 / 1k / 2.5k runs retain zero fitted overlap violations, exact one-frame interaction, and 223 / 252 / 198 potential FPS. VS Code independently verifies 2,690 real nodes with 8–30 radii, zero overlaps on the first 0.5× rendered frame, and a 5.54 px minimum settled gap.

updates.title: Zoom-accurate collisions verified at 2,500 nodes
updates.summary: Visible collision radii now follow the same inverse-square-root zoom compensation as rendering, and zoom-out wakes collision-only settlement without reheating unrelated forces. Clean 500 / 1k / 2.5k runs have zero fitted-view overlap violations at 0.262× / 0.182× / 0.113×, while reaching 221 / 245 / 201 potential FPS with 4.53 / 4.09 / 4.98 ms frame work, exact one-frame target and neighbor response, and zero freezes or teleports. VS Code independently verifies the full 8–30 semantic radius range and zero violations from 0.005× through 64× zoom.

updates.title: One default graph with zoom-stable sizing
updates.summary: Named layouts, Uniform sizing, and Git-history Churn sizing are removed. Connections and File Size now share one 8–30 semantic radius range, while Obsidian-style square-root zoom compensation keeps rendering, arrows, picking, overlays, tooltips, and accessibility bounds aligned. Clean tiny / 500 / 1k / 2.5k potential throughput is 2,775 / 200 / 247 / 153 FPS with 0.36 / 5.00 / 4.06 / 6.55 ms frame work, exact one-frame target and neighbor response, and zero freezes, teleports, or settle violations. VS Code verification also confirms release-time right-click panning and the compact `FPS · ms` HUD.

updates.title: Obsidian sizing and D3 layouts verified
updates.summary: Connections sizing now uses Obsidian's stable square-root curve over unique related nodes. D3 phyllotaxis, centered fixed-axis ranked layouts, and forceRadial rings share the owned physics path. Clean tiny / 500 / 1k / 2.5k potential throughput is 3,239 / 202 / 250 / 157 FPS — 0.2% / 52.9% / 33.1% / 73.0% above M1 — with 0.31 / 4.95 / 4.00 / 6.39 ms frame work, one-frame target and neighbor response, and zero freezes, teleports, or settle violations. In real VS Code, the opt-in HUD stays live after physics settles and follows a vsynced 144 Hz compositor at 142–144 displayed FPS without frame-rate bypass flags.

updates.title: High-refresh presentation gate verified
updates.summary: Headed, vsynced runs on a compositor-backed 165 Hz macOS display measure 161.35 displayed FPS at 500, 163.93 at 1k, and 70.00 at 2.5k, closing both the 144 FPS and sustained 60 FPS gates. Each 500/1k run records one-frame target and neighbor response after correcting the latency boundary to actual input-handler order. Real VS Code independently presents 165.01 FPS with one-frame target/neighbor response, zero freezes or teleports, monotonic settle, imperceptible sleep, and HUD agreement within 1.40%. The display is explicitly documented as virtual because the attached physical panel is limited to 60 Hz; no browser frame-rate or vsync bypass flags were used.

updates.title: Final ladder clears every CPU frame budget
updates.summary: Clean final three-run reports at d6ce971b measure 0.27 / 5.19 / 3.58 / 7.95 ms for tiny / 500 / 1k / 2.5k — 1.15× / 1.46× / 1.49× / 1.39× faster than M1. Potential throughput is 3,720 / 193 / 279 / 126 FPS; the 60 Hz test display presents approximately 60 FPS. Every fixture has one-frame target and neighbor response, zero freezes, teleports, and settle violations, complete imperceptible sleep, and HUD agreement within 0.45%.

updates.title: Semantic style caching removes the largest render cost
updates.summary: Fresh attribution found false props-identity style invalidation costing 1.28 ms/frame at 2.5k. Semantic ref-backed revisions reduce 454–462 rebuilds per run to zero; typed position/edge packing then lowers geometry further. Attributed 2.5k work falls 9.92 → 8.18 ms after caching, and the final unarmed ladder reaches 7.95 ms. Physics is now the dominant 6.29 ms stage; the 2.5k stretch moved 28% toward 6.9 ms without quality-changing approximations.

updates.title: Final real-editor interaction passes
updates.summary: VS Code 1.128.0 in editor view with Files Explorer restored records 144.53 simulation steps/s over exactly 60.00 displayed FPS, 0.36 ms active mean work, one-frame target and neighbor response, zero freezes and teleports, monotonic zero-violation settle, imperceptible sleep, and HUD agreement within 0.61%. The attached screenshots and GIF are final ground-truth presentation evidence.

updates.title: Simulation is decoupled from display cadence
updates.summary: A 144 Hz fixed-timestep accumulator now uses bounded catch-up, at most four substeps, a soft 4 ms simulation budget, and debt reset on sleep/recovery. Clean tiny runs prove decoupling: 143.72 simulation steps/s and 2.38 steps per rendered frame while presentation remains 60.00 FPS and potential throughput is 2,583 FPS. All four tiers pass with one-frame interaction, zero freezes/teleports/settle violations, complete imperceptible sleep, and 0.42% maximum HUD difference.

updates.title: Real editor confirms monotonic settle
updates.summary: VS Code 1.128.0 in editor view with Files Explorer restored recorded 144.43 simulation steps/s across a 60.00 Hz presentation (2.40 steps/frame), proving the loops are independent in-product. The 867-frame drag has one-frame target and neighbor response, zero freezes and teleports, monotonic release energy with zero envelope violations, imperceptible sleep, and HUD agreement within 0.60%.

updates.title: Worker round trip deleted; all clean tiers pass
updates.summary: The measured losing worker, snapshot interpolation, and transferable-buffer implementation is deleted (2,200 lines removed), leaving one synchronous typed physics home and no postMessage interaction path. Clean three-run reports at 1c06ca50 measure 0.49 / 5.59 / 4.22 / 10.03 ms for tiny / 500 / 1k / 2.5k. Every fixture now has one-frame target and neighbor response, zero freezes and teleports, complete settling, and HUD agreement within 0.43%; 500 is 1.35× and 1k is 1.26× faster than M1.

updates.title: Real editor confirms zero-round-trip interaction
updates.summary: VS Code 1.128.0 was rechecked with the graph in an editor and Files Explorer restored. The 983-frame recording has one-frame target and neighbor response, zero freezes and teleports, 0.39 ms active mean frame work, 60.00 displayed FPS on the 60 Hz display, and active HUD agreement within 3.2%. The graph slept imperceptibly; one small settle-envelope rebound remains for M4.

updates.title: Cost attribution chooses main-thread physics
updates.summary: Three-run profiles at clean revision 90540a889 show main-thread physics winning at both decision sizes: 500 improves 7.72 → 5.51 ms (1.40×), neighbor latency 2.33 → 1 frame, and freezes 0.67 → 0; 2.5k improves 11.07 → 10.15 ms (1.09×), neighbor latency 2.67 → 1, and freezes 1 → 0. Worker round trip costs 6.63 ms at 500 and 9.70 ms at 2.5k. M3 will delete the worker/interpolator/transfer path. Next biggest additive costs are physics, then per-frame style + geometry rebuilds.

updates.title: Trustworthy M1 baseline committed
updates.summary: Clean-source schema-v3 baselines now cover tiny, 500, 1k, and 2.5k. Mean drag work is 0.31 / 7.57 / 5.32 / 11.05 ms; potential FPS is 3,232 / 132 / 188 / 90 while headless displayed FPS stays near 60. The baseline exposes the work ahead: 500 misses the 6.9 ms budget, scaled fixtures show 3-frame neighbor latency and one frozen frame on average, and tiny settle has one envelope violation. Teleports are zero and HUD-vs-harness difference is at most 0.94%.

updates.title: Real-editor ground truth captured
updates.summary: VS Code 1.128.0 was verified with the graph in the editor and Files Explorer restored in the sidebar. During an 18-step hub drag, the connected neighbor moved on all 18 captured steps (143 px total), target and neighbor latency were both one frame, freezes and teleports were zero, the HUD matched the corresponding telemetry window within 0.40%, and the final HUD state was Idle. The remaining non-monotonic settle is recorded as a baseline defect.

updates.title: Instrumentation validated
updates.summary: Always-on HUD and harness telemetry now separate CPU potential FPS from elapsed displayed FPS, split simulation and render work, capture exact input-to-render motion and settle behavior, and publish explicit idle state.

updates.title: PR #308 synced and baseline tests passed
updates.summary: The isolated worktree is synced to PR head. Extension and benchmark typechecks and unit-test baselines were green before M1 changes.
